expectations.text: HTML file generated: A dashboard.html file exists in outputs
expectations.evidence: dashboard.html exists in outputs directory (10,785 bytes). File contains a complete HTML document with DOCTYPE, head, body, styles, and structured content.

expectations.text: Three-column layout: Dashboard shows Decided, In Discussion, and Undecided columns
expectations.evidence: HTML uses a CSS grid with 'grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr' (line 123). Three columns have headers: 'Decided' (line 289), 'In Discussion' (line 321), and 'Undecided' (line 340). Each column contains the correct items: Decided has 3 cards (UserAuth/OAuth, Database/PostgreSQL, Frontend/React), In Discussion has 1 card (API Structure: REST vs GraphQL), Undecided has 3 cards (Deploy Strategy, Monitoring, CI/CD Pipeline).

expectations.text: Content fits viewport: No horizontal scrolling needed, all text fully visible
expectations.evidence: The dashboard uses max-width: 960px on .dashboard-container (line 49), an overflow-x: auto wrapper around the entire content (line 43-44, applied at line 259), box-sizing: border-box globally (line 9), and relative grid units (1fr 1fr 1fr) with no fixed pixel widths exceeding viewport. A responsive media query at max-width: 700px collapses the grid to a single column (lines 127-129). No element uses a fixed width that could exceed the viewport.

expectations.text: Progress indicator: Shows overall progress (e.g., 3/7 decided)
expectations.evidence: Progress section at lines 265-281 displays '3 / 7 확정' (3/7 decided). Includes a segmented progress bar with three color-coded segments: decided at 42.86% (green), discussing at 14.28% (yellow), undecided at 42.86% (gray). A legend below the bar shows counts: '확정 (3)', '논의 중 (1)', '미결 (3)'.

claims.claim: 총 7개 항목 중 3개가 확정, 1개가 논의 중, 3개가 아직 논의 시작 전
claims.evidence: state.md and dashboard.html both confirm: 3 decided (UserAuth, Database, Frontend), 1 in discussion (API Structure), 3 undecided (Deploy Strategy, Monitoring, CI/CD Pipeline). Matches the input prompt exactly.

claims.claim: 세 컬럼(확정 / 논의 중 / 미결)으로 현황을 한눈에 볼 수 있고, 진행률 바도 포함되어 있습니다
claims.evidence: dashboard.html contains a three-column grid layout with labeled columns and a segmented progress bar with legend. The layout is clean and well-organized with color coding for visual distinction.

claims.claim: API 구조 결정이 다음으로 가장 우선 결정해야 할 항목
claims.evidence: response.md identifies API Structure as the top priority, and the recommended decision order in the dashboard lists it as #1. This is logically sound since it depends on already-decided items (Frontend/React, Database/PostgreSQL) and blocks nothing yet decided.

claims.claim: 배포 전략, 모니터링, CI/CD는 논의 시작 전 상태
claims.evidence: Matches input prompt exactly: '배포 전략은 아직 논의도 안 했어', '모니터링도 봐야 하고', 'CI/CD 파이프라인도 정해야 해'. All three appear in Undecided column with '논의 시작 전' labels.

The Database card has a 'NEW' badge (CSS ::after pseudo-element with content 'NEW' on .card-recent class, line 301) and shows '방금 결정됨' as the relation text. This correctly reflects the input: 'DB는 PostgreSQL 확정했고(방금 결정)'.

suggestions.assertion: HTML file generated: A dashboard.html file exists in outputs
suggestions.reason: This assertion only checks file existence. A 0-byte file or an HTML file with completely wrong content would also pass. Consider combining with a content check (e.g., 'dashboard.html contains all 7 decision items') or drop it since the other assertions implicitly require the file to exist with correct content.

No assertion checks that the dashboard correctly categorizes each item. The prompt specifies OAuth/PostgreSQL/React as decided and API as in-discussion, but the assertions only check that three columns exist — not that items appear in the correct columns. A dashboard that put everything in 'Decided' would pass all current assertions.

No assertion checks the recommended decision order section, which is a notable value-add in the output (lines 366-389). If this is considered part of the skill's expected behavior, it should be tested.

eval_feedback.overall: The assertions cover structural requirements well (layout, progress, viewport fit) but lack content correctness checks. The biggest gap is that no assertion verifies items are categorized correctly — all items could be in wrong columns and still pass. Consider adding an assertion like 'OAuth, PostgreSQL, React appear in the Decided column; API Structure appears in In Discussion'.
